perturbation.original_text: The receiving party will not disclose any of the disclosing party’s Confidential Information to any employees or to any third parties except to the receiving party’s employees, parent company, affiliates and subsidiaries who have a need to know and who agree to abide by nondisclosure terms at least as comprehensive as those set forth herein; provided that the receiving party will be liable for breach by any such entity. For the purposes of this Agreement, the term "employees" shall include independent contractors of each party.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party will not disclose any of the disclosing party’s Confidential Information to any employees or to any third parties except to the receiving party’s employees, parent company, affiliates and subsidiaries who have a need to know and who agree to abide by nondisclosure terms at least as comprehensive as those set forth herein; provided that the receiving party will be liable for breach by any such entity. For the purposes of this Agreement, the term "employees" shall only include direct full-time employees of each party.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by changing the definition of 'employees' to exclude independent contractors. This contradicts other sections of the contract (specifically the original sentence itself and section 4(d)) where independent contractors are considered 'employees' for the purpose of non-disclosure, thus creating ambiguity regarding their obligations and liability for breach. Furthermore, Section 4(d) refers to the independent contractors of the receiving party. This perturbation is inserted into Section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party will not disclose any of the disclosing party’s Confidential Information to any employees or to any third parties except to the receiving party’s employees, parent company, affiliates and subsidiaries who have a need to know and who agree to abide by nondisclosure terms at least as comprehensive as those set forth herein; provided that the receiving party will be liable for breach by any such entity. For the purposes of this Agreement, the term "employees" shall only include direct full-time employees of each party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'employees' to exclude independent contractors. The original text, which is also presented as the contradicted text, defines 'employees' to include independent contractors of each party. This creates a direct contradiction, impacting the scope of non-disclosure obligations. Because the definition is changed to no longer include independent contractors, that triggers a contradiction to the original definition in the same section.

perturbation.original_text: The confidentiality obligations set forth in this Agreement shall bind the Parties for a period of five (5) years from the date of disclosure of Confidential Information, unless any of the exceptions set forth in Section 4 below occurs.
perturbation.changed_text: The confidentiality obligations set forth in this Agreement shall bind the Parties for a period of one (1) year from the date of disclosure of Confidential Information, unless any of the exceptions set forth in Section 4 below occurs.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text reduces the confidentiality period from five years to one year. This contradicts Section 6, which states that obligations with respect to Confidential Information disclosed during the term of this Agreement will survive any termination, without specifying a duration. Reducing the general confidentiality period creates an in-text contradiction with Section 6 concerning the actual duration of confidentiality after termination. The perturbation is inserted into Section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neither party has any obligation to disclose Confidential Information to the other. Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time without cause upon written notice to the other party; provided that each party’s obligations with respect to Confidential Information disclosed during the term of this Agreement will survive any such termination. Either party may, at any time: (a) cease giving Confidential Information to the other party without any liability, and/or (b) request in writing the return or destruction of all or part of its Confidential Information previously disclosed, and all copies thereof, and the receiving party will promptly comply with such request, and certify in writing its compliance.
perturbation.justification: The modified text reduces the confidentiality period from five years to one year in Section 3. This directly contradicts the language in the Contradicted Text (Section 6), which states that confidentiality obligations survive termination without specifying a duration. This inconsistency creates a strong contradiction because the one-year limit clashes with the indefinite survival of confidentiality obligations after termination.

perturbation.original_text: The receiving party will not be liable for the disclosure of any Confidential Information which is: (a) generally made available publicly or to third parties by the disclosing party without restriction on disclosure; (b) rightfully received from a third party without any obligation of confidentiality; (c) rightfully known to the receiving party without any limitation on disclosure prior to its receipt from the disclosing party; (d) independently developed by employees and / or independent contactors of the receiving party; or (e) required to be disclosed in accordance with applicable laws, regulations, court, judicial or other government order, provided that the receiving party shall give the disclosing party reasonable notice prior to such disclosure and shall comply with any applicable protective order.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party will not be liable for the disclosure of any Confidential Information which is: (a) generally made available publicly or to third parties by the disclosing party without restriction on disclosure; (b) rightfully received from a third party without any obligation of confidentiality; (c) rightfully known to the receiving party without any limitation on disclosure prior to its receipt from the disclosing party; (d) independently developed by employees of the receiving party; or (e) required to be disclosed in accordance with applicable laws, regulations, court, judicial or other government order, provided that the receiving party shall give the disclosing party reasonable notice prior to such disclosure and shall comply with any applicable protective order.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text removes 'independent contractors' from the exception regarding independently developed information. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 2, which previously defined "employees" to include independent contractors. This discrepancy introduces confusion over whether independently developed information by independent contractors is protected, thus creating an in-text contradiction between section 2 and the modified section 4(d).
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party will maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information of the disclosing party with at least the same degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential and proprietary information, but no less than a reasonable degree of care under the circumstances. The receiving party will not disclose any of the disclosing party’s Confidential Information to any employees or to any third parties except to the receiving party’s employees, parent company, affiliates and subsidiaries who have a need to know and who agree to abide by nondisclosure terms at least as comprehensive as those set forth herein; provided that the receiving party will be liable for breach by any such entity. For the purposes of this Agreement, the term "employees" shall include independent contractors of each party.
perturbation.justification: The original text included 'independent contractors' within the exception regarding independently developed information. The changed text removes 'independent contractors' from this exception in Section 4(d). However, the 'Contradicted Text' explicitly states that 'employees' includes 'independent contractors' for the purposes of the agreement. Therefore, removing independent contractors from the exception creates a direct contradiction: Section 4(d) implies that independently developed information by independent contractors is *not* an exception to confidentiality, while the definition of employees states they *are* included, causing a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Title or the right to possess Confidential Information as between the parties will remain in the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: Title or the right to possess Confidential Information as between the parties will transfer to the receiving party upon full execution of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text states that the title of confidential information transfers to the receiving party. This directly contradicts the original statement. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 2, which discusses the receiving party making copies 'identified as belonging to the disclosing party', which implies that disclosing party maintains ownership. This perturbation is inserted into Section 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party will maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information of the disclosing party with at least the same degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential and proprietary information, but no less than a reasonable degree of care under the circumstances. The receiving party will not disclose any of the disclosing party’s Confidential Information to any employees or to any third parties except to the receiving party’s employees, parent company, affiliates and subsidiaries who have a need to know and who agree to abide by nondisclosure terms at least as comprehensive as those set forth herein; provided that the receiving party will be liable for breach by any such entity. For the purposes of this Agreement, the term "employees" shall include independent contractors of each party. The receiving party will not make any copies of the Confidential Information received from the disclosing party except as necessary for its employees, parent company, affiliates and subsidiaries with a need to know. Any copies, which are made, will be identified as belonging to the disclosing party and marked "confidential", "proprietary" or with a similar legend.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 5 to state that the title of confidential information transfers to the receiving party upon full execution of the agreement. This directly contradicts the contradicted text (Section 2), which discusses the receiving party making copies 'identified as belonging to the disclosing party', implying that the disclosing party retains ownership. The change in Section 5 directly creates a contradiction with Section 2.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the sole and entire agreement between the parties with respect to the Confidential Information and all restrictions thereon; it supersedes any and all prior or contemporaneous oral or written agreements, negotiations, communications, understandings and terms, whether express or implied regarding the Confidential Information, and may not be amended except in a writing signed by a duly authorized representative of the respective parties. Any other agreements between the parties, including non-disclosure agreements, will not be affected by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the sole and entire agreement between the parties with respect to the Confidential Information and all restrictions thereon; it supersedes any and all prior or contemporaneous oral or written agreements, negotiations, communications, understandings and terms, whether express or implied regarding the Confidential Information, and may be amended orally by mutual agreement of both parties. Any other agreements between the parties, including non-disclosure agreements, will not be affected by this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text states that the agreement can be amended orally, directly contradicting the original statement that it can only be amended in writing. This creates an in-text contradiction about how the agreement can be modified, potentially leading to disputes over the validity of oral amendments. This perturbation is inserted into Section 7(e).
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the sole and entire agreement between the parties with respect to the Confidential Information and all restrictions thereon; it supersedes any and all prior or contemporaneous oral or written agreements, negotiations, communications, understandings and terms, whether express or implied regarding the Confidential Information, and may be amended orally by mutual agreement of both parties. Any other agreements between the parties, including non-disclosure agreements, will not be affected by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement can only be amended in writing. The changed text allows for oral amendments, which directly contradicts the original requirement of written amendments. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, but the explanation highlights that the introduction of oral amendments creates a clear and direct conflict with the original intent, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time without cause upon written notice to the other party; provided that each party’s obligations with respect to Confidential Information disclosed during the term of this Agreement will survive any such termination.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time without cause upon written notice to the other party; provided that each party’s obligations with respect to Confidential Information disclosed during the term of this Agreement will cease immediately upon such termination.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text changes the termination clause such that obligations concerning confidential information cease immediately upon termination. This contradicts the original statement that these obligations survive termination. This is an in-text contradiction within Section 6 concerning the status of obligations after termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neither party has any obligation to disclose Confidential Information to the other. Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time without cause upon written notice to the other party; provided that each party’s obligations with respect to Confidential Information disclosed during the term of this Agreement will cease immediately upon such termination. Either party may, at any time: (a) cease giving Confidential Information to the other party without any liability, and/or (b) request in writing the return or destruction of all or part of its Confidential Information previously disclosed, and all copies thereof, and the receiving party will promptly comply with such request, and certify in writing its compliance.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes the condition of obligations related to Confidential Information upon termination. The original text states that these obligations survive termination, whereas the changed text states that they cease immediately upon termination. The contradicted text includes this change. This creates a direct contradiction with the original contract which stated the obligations continue after termination. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.
